reasoning: Lilly King's 100m breaststroke record (1:04.13) has stood since 2017. While there was a close challenge by Tang Qianting in early 2024 (1:04.39), the times at the 2024 Paris Olympics were notably slower (the winning time was 1:05.28), partly attributed to a shallower pool. Going into the post-Olympic year (2025), there is typically less peak conditioning, although the 2025 World Championships in Singapore will offer a strong venue for attempts. Ruta Meilutyte, who swam closely in 2023, struggled with form in 2024. Therefore, without a crowd of competitors consistently swimming in the low 1:04s, the record is favored to survive the 2025 season and reach early 2026. Taking the base rate of record preservation and current competitive landscape into account, I estimate a roughly 78% chance that she still holds it.
